title: Bitter Dutch Peas Stamppot
description: A traditional Dutch dish made with split peas, kale, and smoked bacon, served with a side of rye bread. This recipe is a keto-friendly twist on a classic monsoon-inspired meal.
instructions: Combine split peas and water in a large saucepan and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 40 minutes, or until the peas are tender.
Add chopped kale and smoked bacon to the saucepan and stir to combine.
In a separate pan, melt butter over medium heat and saut6 onion until softened.
Add garlic to the pan and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Stir the onion and garlic mixture into the saucepan with the peas and kale.
Season with salt, black pepper, and additional bacon if desired.
Serve the stamppot hot with a side of rye bread.
Reheat leftovers in a bain-marie or microwave-safe dish for up to 2 days.
To make a more intense flavor, add more smoked bacon or a few tablespoons of bacon fat to the saucepan during the last 10 minutes of cooking.
Adjust the seasoning as needed before serving.
Leftovers can be refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 2 months.
Reheat in a saucepan or microwave-safe dish, adding a splash of water if needed to prevent drying out.
